item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, somebody actually made a llama-wearing-galoshes costume, and had two people get into it, specifically for the SCP article. Look, the hazard tape on the floor has the Foundation emblem on it!!!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Filename: Larry the Loving Llama-new.jpg
Author: サメのお腹は液晶でいっぱい, Elogee FishTruck
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: SCP Foundation Wiki
Derivative of:

Name: リャマの着ぐるみはライブ感との戦い～SCP-1545 リャマラブラリー製作ログその③～
Author: サメのお腹は液晶でいっぱい
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Source Link: note

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was sifting through random SCP posts and when I came upon this, I laughed out loud at the picture. Perfect mixture of creepy and hilarious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, this is both hilarious and really fucking terrifying. I remember seeing this on The Volgun's channel a while back, and I'm still pretty wigged out by it. Well fucking done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go Lonwood Llamas!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably one of the best SCPs on here. The spooky/jokey aspects are blended together so well.

item.title: caAArl no sleeping on the job again
item.content:encoded:
I had a feeling this was going to be about some goofy creepy stuff because of the picture, but didn't expect people happily dragging dead corpses around in a suit reeking of piss, shit and blood, the fuck +1

Also, this makes me think of "Llamas with hats" because of the whole llama and death thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
500th +1?
Why yes, don't mind if I do.

I like the cross reference being added, but the skip earned its 500 without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you aware that D-5484 is dead, Larry?
He's just taking a nap, doctor.

I don't know what it is, but imagining this exchange with a dehydraded man doing a Barney impression in that llama costume (dessicated and befouled after all the people who have died in it) with the rear dragging due to a DEAD man being inside of it) is one of the creepiest things I've read on this site. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was so rediculous and executed so well that I had to give it a +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Be me.
Read SCPs
Click random SCP.
This.
See photo.
Stop reading SCPs.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

I remember this article being one of the first pieces I read on the site after I joined way back in the day, so getting assigned to come up with a crosslink for this one is a real treat. One of my favorite mentions of SCP-1545 is in SCP-1296, which not only was hilarious, but indicates that SCPs with effects on llamas likely will also have an effect on 1545. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1545 is to be kept in Containment Locker 1545 at Site 40, with access barred to all personnel with a clearance level under three (3). No other containment procedures are necessary at this time.

To this:

SCP-1545 is to be kept in Containment Locker 1545 at Site 40, with access barred to all personnel with a clearance level under three (3). Following Incident 1296-1545, testing of SCP-1545 with additional anomalous objects requires approval of personnel with a clearance level of four (4) on a case by case basis. No other containment procedures are necessary at this time.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I think I've found a new favourite. Emphatic +1, solid article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh My God that is terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The phrase "my bum is a bit sluggish today" gets my upvote. Also the concept of a Llama dragging itself with a corpse in there body is really creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … WHY HAVE I NOT UPVOTED THIS YET?!? I've been bumping people to this SCP as an example of weirdness done right!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had previously only seen this through SCP memes on tumblr. I didn't know that it was a real article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's such a refreshingly direct thing to see in a writeup!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first Scp I have ever read and I think that the Scp is great. Even the title, "Larry the loving llama" is just boss.
"Larry the Upvote Llama"

item.title: What?
item.content:encoded: This is soooooooo weird, but that's what makes Scp's cool :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its so sad but so hilarious XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, there's some articles that make me sad I cannot upvote multiple times. It deserves one for the image, one for the description, and one for the interview logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like the Dancing Plague except a million times worse. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strange, but interesting. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucked up and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "You are a terrible person. A terrible person. We weren't even testing for that." -GlaDOS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the rearward person performing various jigs.

Instant upvote. Absolutely nothing past this point could possibly change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant. This made my day. Don't change anything, perfect just the way it is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmmmm yes perfect headcanon accepted

Gosh, this SCP. I think this is a perfect example of less is more. It's very concise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: !!! this is a very good explanation, helps me appreciate the psychological impact of this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's not how I read it at all. I perceived it as the guy in the front of the suit doing his best to sustain the illusion. He knows he's a person, he knows the guy in the back of the suit is a person, he knows that Larry is just a costume… but the fundamental priority on his Maslovian hierarchy is "sustain the illusion of Larry being animate".

Imagine if Larry was non-anomalous, and the people inside Larry were well-paid professionals, and a small child said "what about the people inside you?"

The actor in the front would say "oh, those are just my helpers! Would you like to see me dance?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This went from hilarious to horrifying the second I read the interview. The article, at first, makes it seem that the subjects become Larry, but then it's revealed that the subjects are trapped "helpers" and Larry is actually a sentient being using them until they succumb to emaciation. This supremely cruel behavior in contrast to his vocal need to make everyone happy is serious nightmare fuel! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://imgur.com/gallery/PpKiJIS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I snorted throughout the entire article. And felt awful for it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed I named the D-class in the second interview James without realizing it. FFFFFFFFFFFFF

item.title: Aha
item.content:encoded:
I've figured it out. I've figured out what those two people were doing in the costume — why they got in it in the first place.

They were using Larry as a recreational substance. The only problem was, they forgot to get a trip sitter.

Think how much fun it would be, to be, to truly be Larry the Loving Llama with all your heart and soul! To frolic and cavort, to dance and play and make the children laugh! And then, after a few hours, someone restrains you and pulls you out of the costume, and everything's fine! You don't even have to worry about hangovers or drug tests!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fucking llama is creepy. How did this happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good stuff.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded:
Seems that Larry can cause a faster death amongst D-Class than one would initially think.

I think the Foundation would stop testing just out of mercy.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: Neither, you keep vomiting and retching whenever the smell hits your nostrils.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: once you get in Larry and your mind is affected, do you enjoy the smell? Or does the stench just get relegated to a barely-relevant minor detail?

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: …. I think this is one of my new favorite entries. +1

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: They don't launder it because they don't want to risk it breaking, so the foundation just has this fettering, pussy, piss-filled costume lying in the containment locker. Larry is known for having the highest rate of puking at first observation, even more than some SCPs that anomalously cause vomiting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck.

I'll have to think about this one, leaning towards +1.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: why D:

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: in all seriousness: if Larry can be laundered, shouldn't that be in the containment procedures?

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: Larry can be llaundered.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: oh my god this explains everything

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: WHAT THE FUCK KIND OF HIGH SCHOOL DID YOU GO TO

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded:
…oh god.

OH GOD
OH FUCKING GOD

I AM HAVING FLASHBACKS TO THE RESTROOMS IN HIGH SCHOOL.

OH GOD WHAT IF LARRY HAS A GLORY HOLE. ;_;

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: So is Larry, the most horrible thing in the world?

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded:
…
You forgot about the vomit from other people who smelled it.

item.title: Re: You want horror?
item.content:encoded: …I think I'm going to be sick…

item.title: You want horror?
item.content:encoded:
Imagine what Larry smells like.

Imagine all the people who've put Larry on, over the years. Imagine how they've sweated as they dance merrily around in the summer heat… for hours.

Imagine how they've pissed themselves, and shat themselves, and kept on dancing. That caked-on muck of piss and shit and sweat, clinging to their skin, as they dance and frolic. Imagine the smell as it dries, and tightens on the skin, and the skin starts to rupture. Blood oozes in some places, trickles in others, and outright spurts in still others.

Meanwhile, the feet are chafing, and blisters are beginning to form. Very quickly, infection sets in.

Imagine the smell. Yeast, and portable toilets, and blood, and rotting meat, and pus….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just imagine the guy in the butt slowly dancing after the guy in front dies and they can't move any more. Dancing slower and slower. *shudder*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well you didn't think "forcefully removed" meant researchers pulled them out, did you?

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3BAyANk_D8U&feature=player_detailpage#t=2s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… I found Larry…

http://i.imgur.com/B25gH.gif - Not sure if NSFW, it does show a bull penis. User discretion is advised.
